title: World Cup traffic creates long lines but stays organized, easing citywide fears
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: World Cup Months of planning went into figuring out how to transport thousands of fans from the city to Boston Stadium in Foxborough.
Scottish soccer supporters arrive at Foxborough Station ahead of the 2026 World Cup match between Scotland and Haiti at Boston Stadium.

Erin Clark/The Boston Globe By Darin Zullo June 14, 2026 | 11:18 AM 1 minute to read For a city like Boston, transportation was always going to be one of the main stress points when hosting the World Cup.

title: Man shot and killed in dining room at Cape Cod restaurant
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Crime The restaurant’s owner said she doesn’t know the victim but offered her condolences to his family on social media.
By Darin Zullo June 14, 2026 | 10:29 AM 1 minute to read A man at a restaurant in Dennis was fatally shot early Saturday, turning a dining room into a crime scene.
The victim was shot and killed at around midnight, according to the Cape & Islands District Attorney’s Office.

title: Boston cop rallies ecstatic World Cup crowd with soccer juggling skills
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: World Cup A video of the cop juggling a soccer ball for an energized crowd of World Cup fans has gone viral on social media.
World Cup fans watch a game at Boston's Fan Fest in City Hall Plaza on Saturday.
Finn Gomez/The Boston Globe By Darin Zullo June 14, 2026 | 9:28 AM 1 minute to read As thousands of World Cup fans swarmed Boston on Saturday, one city cop decided to play ball.

title: Boy, 14, arrested for armed robbery of kids’ lemonade stand in Southie
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Local News Boston police are still searching for a second suspect in connection with the incident.
South Boston residents Jenn and David Byrne, whose children were robbed at their lemonade stand on Wednesday, say they are still in shock.

Ariela Lopez for The Boston Globe A 14-year-old boy was arrested Friday in South Boston in connection with the armed robbery of a children’s lemonade stand earlier in the week, according to Boston police.

title: A wild night in Foxborough shows Boston is ready to embrace magic of World Cup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: World Cup The din of bagpipes, The Proclaimers, and decibel-defying chants rang across Foxborough on Saturday night.
Thousands of Scotland fans packed into Foxborough on Saturday for World Cup action.
Erin Clark/Globe Staff By Conor Ryan June 14, 2026 | 7:30 AM 4 minutes to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Ceddanne Rafaela continues breakout year offensively with 2 RBIs Saturday
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Boston Red Sox Rafaela has been worth every bit of his 8-year, $50 million contract.
Ceddanne Rafaela has 8 RBIs in his last three games.

(AP Photo/Jim Davis) For all the talk about how much money Brayan Bello and Kristian Campbell are making to play in Worcester, Ceddanne Rafaela is a great example of why signing players early can be well worth the risk.

title: Watch: Scotland fans sing anthem together before World Cup game at Boston Stadium
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: World Cup Thousands of Scotland fans belted out their anthem before Saturday's match against Haiti.
Scotland fans sang as one for "Flower of Scotland" on Saturday at Boston Stadium.
(AP Photo/Martin Meissner) FOXBOROUGH — Saturday night marked the first time that Scotland took part in World Cup action in 28 years.

title: Red Sox secure first home series win since early April with another win over Rangers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox The last time Boston recorded a series win at Fenway was on April 8 against the Milwaukee Brewers.
Home wins have been hard to come by for the Red Sox.
Jaiden Tripi/Getty Images The Boston Red Sox achieved something that they hadn’t done in over two months on Saturday.

title: The best videos of Scottish, Haitian fans taking over Boston for World Cup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: World Cup Thousands of Scottish and Haitian soccer fans have taken over Boston over the last week — celebrating with locals, commandeering bars, and making treks down the "Cop Slide".
Scottish fans has descended upon Boston over the past week.
Craig F.

title: Scotland fan completes 3,000-mile walk to Boston to watch World Cup opener
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: World Cup Craig Ferguson walked from Los Angeles to Boston and raised over $1 million for men's mental health.
Craig Ferguson walked from LA to Boston for charity.
(Matt Porter/Globe Staff) Craig Ferguson has completed his 3,000 mile trek to Boston ahead of Scotland’s World Cup opening match with Haiti on Saturday.
